reasoning: Any nonzero u64 has at least one set bit — this is a fundamental mathematical fact. The lemma is fully verified (not external_body), so this is a correct and expected consequence.
With `a != 0` and `a >> 1 == 0`, the only possibility is `a == 1` with bit 0 set. The lemma returns `i < 1`, forcing `i == 0`. This is correct boundary behavior, not a spec gap.
The ensures gives `i < hi` and the requires gives `hi <= 64`, so `i < 64` follows trivially by transitivity. This is a correct and expected logical consequence.
